PanderThings live in a different economy from social media and smartphones — slower, more local, and a whole lot more fun on a proper screen. You can see most of this on your phone, but you’ll be missing the best parts: the clicky‑movey bits, the audio, the musical speed bumps. Take it home and open it on a laptop or computer.

In the meantime: share it in person. AirDrop (iPhone) or Quick Share (Android) to someone right next to you.

If you’d like to lessen your fidgety phone dependency [all of us these days] and learn to slow down a bit … you might try our Bach Bees Playlist.

193 S. Main Street  •  Russellville, KY

“Your daily dose of possibilities”

Click any panel to open  →
tap a panel →
01
Pink striped door  •  the story
01
A corner of kindness
The Flying Pig Coffee Shoppe

In a restored 1875 building on South Main Street in Russellville, KY, the Flying Pig Coffee Shoppe is a community-focused coffee house with a cozy country-chic atmosphere, a whimsical spiral staircase to a loft, and a pink striped door that is always, always open.

Owner Tammy Wellman took a leap of faith during the pandemic and bought this beloved local institution in 2021. She serves Bongo Java organic fair-trade coffee and milk from Chaney’s Dairy. She feeds people. She hosts painters. She sends care packages to troops abroad.

The Flying Pig was nominated for Logan County’s 2024 Small Business of the Year. 

Not for social media. For people.

02
More than coffee  •  arts & community
02
More than coffee
Arts & Community

The Flying Pig hosts painting nights where Russellville folk discover they’re artists. There’s a sign out front that says Strong Like Our Coffee — a promise that turns out to apply to the people, not just the brew.

Tammy runs free community dinners for Halloween and Thanksgiving. She organises care packages for troops. She supports the Humane Society of Logan County. She takes up causes other people quietly step around. She does all of this with love — which is, it turns out, the only ingredient that scales.

“Love Jesus, Shop Local, Be Kind.”

She also emphasises the arts in ways some cynical people said Logan County would never go for. Tammy proves them wrong, with a loving yet smart attitude, every single week. 

Please don’t post this on social media. It was made for a different economy.

03
Jumped out of planes  •  the owner
03
Go big or go home
About Tammy

Tammy Wellman came to Kentucky from Nashville, where she’d spent years touring the country as a musician. She’s jumped out of planes. She’s climbed Mt. Fuji. She’s swum with sharks. She holds a Business Management degree from WKU and spent years managing medical programmes at Vanderbilt — including one that helped create the world’s first artificial kidney.

She is a single mum to three boys who test her recipes. She lives in Adairville, just down the road, and would take twinkling stars over city lights, any day.

“Somehow, buying a coffee shop is the scariest thing I’ve ever done. But I wasn’t going to spend the rest of my life wondering.”

If God was going to send her a door, she figured it would be pink striped. She was right. 

PanderThings travel by email, by hand, in parks and groceries. The algorithm doesn’t need to know about this one.

The pink door is always open

Be it a caffeine fix, a shoulder to cry on, or happiness in the form of carbs — Tammy’s got you.

The village of customers and staff at the Flying Pig is real. Come add yourself to it. 

This lives in a social economy that works differently from social media. Slower. More local. Worth trying.

05 Worth knowing about
The Star in the P
What is a PanderThing?
05
Worth knowing about
The Star in the P

This document is a PanderThing — a self-contained file that lives entirely on your device. No internet required. No tracking. No cookies. It is a document, not a trap.

PanderThings travel by email, by hand, in parks and groceries. They belong to a social economy that works differently from social media — slower, more local, more honest.

Every PanderThing includes a locomprotion: a genuine word of support for a neighbour. Not advertising. The real thing. This one is for Robyn’s Nest, right next door at 191 South Main.

PanderThings are made by Thoth Industries, invented in Lewisburg, Logan County, Kentucky, with a fake bird. If you’d like one for your business: teach.the.conflicts@gmail.com • (704) 728-0885 — text first with a good time to call. 

We’d rather this got passed person to person than posted. Email it to someone. Print it out. Leave it somewhere interesting.

06
Next door at 191  •  Robyn’s Nest
06
Locomprotion — a little loco
Robyn’s Nest Boutique

Right next door at 191 S. Main St. is Robyn’s Nest Boutique — a family-owned shop full of curated women’s fashion, accessories, and the kind of personal touch you only get when the owner is genuinely present.

Robyn is a real, genuine woman — not afraid to post a photo of herself frustrated and describe the honest hardship of keeping a ma-and-pop shop alive in the 2020s. She works hard. She cares about her community. She is part of the revitalised downtown Russellville scene that the Flying Pig helped create.

y’all can’t run a boutique without no clothes hangers.
— Robyn, keeping it honest

191 S. Main St., Russellville KY
(270) 543-5127 • sammies123.rw@gmail.com 

PanderThings belong to a kind of social economy that predates the feed and will outlast it. You’re already in it.
